description: This subject focuses on systematic and structured approaches to software development, including lifecycle models, requirement engineering, system design, estimation, and project management.

chapters.description: Covers different models used to develop software systems in a structured manner.

topics.description: Software Development Life Cycle models define how software is developed step by step.

subtopics.name: Waterfall Model
subtopics.details: Development flows in fixed phases such as requirement, design, coding, testing, and maintenance. Each phase must be completed before moving forward. It is simple but not flexible for changing requirements.

subtopics.name: Agile Model
subtopics.details: Software is developed in small cycles called sprints. Continuous feedback is taken, and changes can be easily incorporated. It is widely used in modern development.

subtopics.name: Spiral Model
subtopics.details: Each iteration involves planning, risk analysis, development, and evaluation. It is suitable for complex and high-risk projects.

chapters.description: Focuses on gathering, analyzing, and documenting system requirements.

subtopics.name: Functional Requirements
subtopics.details: Examples include user login, assignment submission, data processing, and report generation.

subtopics.name: Non Functional Requirements
subtopics.details: Includes performance, security, scalability, and usability requirements.

subtopics.name: Structure of SRS
subtopics.details: Includes introduction, overall description, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, and external interfaces.

chapters.description: Focuses on planning and managing software projects.

subtopics.name: Gantt Chart
subtopics.details: Displays project tasks against time, showing start and end dates.

subtopics.name: PERT and CPM
subtopics.details: Used to determine critical path and optimize project duration.

description: Covers advanced networking concepts such as routing, IP addressing, transport protocols, and wireless communication.

chapters.description: Explains how the internet is structured and operates.

topics.description: Method to uniquely identify devices on a network.

subtopics.name: IPv4
subtopics.details: Uses dotted decimal format and supports limited address space.

subtopics.name: IPv6
subtopics.details: Provides a larger address space and improved routing efficiency.

subtopics.name: Subnet Masking
subtopics.details: Improves network performance and security by organizing IP addresses into sub-networks.

chapters.description: Protocols used to determine best path for data transfer.

subtopics.name: RIP
subtopics.details: Uses hop count to determine best path and updates routing tables periodically.

subtopics.name: OSPF
subtopics.details: Uses Dijkstra algorithm to calculate shortest path and maintains full topology map.

description: Focuses on managing system resources such as CPU, memory, and processes.

topics.description: States through which a process passes.

subtopics.name: Process States
subtopics.details: Includes new, ready, running, waiting, and terminated states.

subtopics.name: FCFS
subtopics.details: Processes are executed in order of arrival.

subtopics.name: Round Robin
subtopics.details: Each process gets equal CPU time in cyclic order.

description: Focuses on startup development, business planning, and entrepreneurial skills.

subtopics.name: Leadership
subtopics.details: Helps in decision making and managing business operations.

subtopics.name: Risk Taking
subtopics.details: Entrepreneurs must take calculated risks to achieve success.

description: Focuses on research, topic selection, and project planning.

subtopics.name: Research Papers
subtopics.details: Helps identify research gaps and define project scope.

description: Provides real-world industrial exposure and practical experience.

subtopics.name: Team Collaboration
subtopics.details: Improves communication, coordination, and real-world problem-solving skills.

description: Focuses on building interactive web applications using JavaScript and modern frontend frameworks like React.

chapters.description: Covers basic programming concepts using JavaScript.

topics.description: Basic building blocks of JavaScript programming.

subtopics.name: Variables and Data Types
subtopics.details: JavaScript supports data types such as number, string, boolean, object, and arrays. Variables are declared using var, let, and const.

subtopics.name: Functions
subtopics.details: Functions allow modular programming. They can take inputs, process logic, and return outputs.

subtopics.name: Document Object Model
subtopics.details: JavaScript can access and modify HTML elements using DOM methods like getElementById and querySelector.

subtopics.name: Event Handling
subtopics.details: Events like click, submit, and keypress trigger JavaScript functions to make pages interactive.

chapters.description: Modern JavaScript library for building UI components.

subtopics.name: Functional Components
subtopics.details: They use hooks such as useState and useEffect to manage state and lifecycle.

subtopics.name: useState Hook
subtopics.details: Allows components to store and update data dynamically.

description: Focuses on developing mobile applications using Android platform and modern development tools.

subtopics.name: Android Components
subtopics.details: Includes activities, services, broadcast receivers, and content providers.

subtopics.name: Activity Lifecycle
subtopics.details: Includes onCreate, onStart, onResume, onPause, onStop, and onDestroy.

subtopics.name: XML Layouts
subtopics.details: Defines structure of user interface including buttons, text views, and layouts.

description: Covers methods and techniques used to verify and validate software systems.

subtopics.name: Verification
subtopics.details: Ensures software is built correctly according to design specifications.

subtopics.name: Validation
subtopics.details: Ensures the final product meets user requirements.

subtopics.name: Boundary Value Analysis
subtopics.details: Focuses on input boundaries where errors are most likely.

description: Focuses on cybersecurity, ethical hacking, and digital investigation techniques.

subtopics.name: Malware
subtopics.details: Includes viruses, worms, and trojans that damage or exploit systems.

subtopics.name: Reconnaissance
subtopics.details: Used to collect data about target systems before attack.

description: Focuses on principles of management, team handling, and project execution.

subtopics.name: Planning
subtopics.details: Defines objectives and strategies to achieve them.

subtopics.name: Organizing
subtopics.details: Allocates tasks and resources efficiently.

description: Final project integrating knowledge from all subjects to build a complete system.

subtopics.name: Frontend Development
subtopics.details: Includes building UI using frameworks like React.

subtopics.name: Backend Development
subtopics.details: Handles APIs, database, and business logic.

subtopics.name: Unit Testing
subtopics.details: Each component is tested independently.
